subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: get help on meteor command line usage

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: run a meteor development server in the current project. Searches upward from the current directory for the root directory of a Meteor project

options.description: if you have added a platform to your app with 'meteor add-platform', you can pass one of the following targets as an argument to this command
port to listen on (instead of the default 3000)
enable server-side debugging via debugging clients like the Node.js command-line debugger, Chrome DevTools, or Visual Studio Code
enable server-side debugging via debugging clients like the Node.js command-line debugger, Chrome DevTools, or Visual Studio Code. The server will be paused at
location where mobile builds connect to the Meteor server. Defaults to your local IP and the port that the Meteor server binds to. Can include a URL scheme (for
local port where Cordova will serve the content. It's important when multiple Cordova apps are build from the same Meteor app source code as by default the port
simulate production mode. Minify and bundle CSS and JS files
run without parsing logs from stdout and stderr
set optional data for Meteor.settings on the server
specify the release of Meteor to use
print all output from builds logs
don't run linters used by the app on every rebuild
don't run the release updater to check for new releases
allow packages in your project to be upgraded or downgraded to versions that are potentially incompatible with the current versions, if required to satisfy all
run with additional packages (comma separated, for example: --extra-packages "package-name1, package-name2@1.2.3")
don't create bundles for certain web architectures (comma separated, for example: --exclude-archs "web.browser.legacy, web.cordova")

subcommands.name: debug
subcommands.description: dEPRECATED. The 'meteor debug' command is deprecated in favor of 'meteor --inspect-brk'

subcommands.name: create
subcommands.description: create a new Meteor project. By default, it uses React and makes a subdirectory named name and copies in the template app. You can pass an absolute or relative

options.description: specify the release of Meteor to use
create a new meteor package instead of an app
example template to use
show list of available examples
create an empty app
create an app with as few Meteor packages as possible
create a fully scaffolded app
create a basic react-based app, same as default
create a basic vue-based app
create a basic apollo-based app
create a basic svelte-based app
create a basic Typescript React-based app
create a basic blaze-based app
create a basic react-based app, with tailwind configured

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: attempts to bring you to the latest version of Meteor, and then to upgrade your packages to their latest versions. By default, update will not break compatibili

options.description: update the package versions only. Do not update the release
update the release to a patch release only
update to a specific release of meteor
allow packages in your project to be upgraded or downgraded to versions that are potentially incompatible with the current versions, if required to satisfy all
update all packages including indirect dependencies

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: add packages to your Meteor project. You can add multiple packages with one command

options.description: allow packages in your project to be upgraded or downgraded to versions that are potentially incompatible with the current versions, if required to satisfy all

subcommands.name: remove
subcommands.description: removes a package previously added to your Meteor project. You can remove multiple packages with one command. For a list of the packages that your application i

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: lists the packages that you have explicitly added to your project

options.description: outputs a tree showing how packages are referenced
outputs the tree in JSON format
show weakly referenced dependencies in the tree
adds more package details to the JSON output

subcommands.name: add-platform
subcommands.description: adds platforms to your Meteor project. You can add multiple platforms with one command

subcommands.name: remove-platform
subcommands.description: removes a platform previously added to your Meteor project

subcommands.name: list-platforms
subcommands.description: lists all of the platforms added to your project

subcommands.name: ensure-cordova-dependencies
subcommands.description: check if the dependencies are installed, otherwise install them

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: package this project up for deployment. The command outputs a directory with builds for all platforms in this project

options.description: build in debug mode (don't minify, etc)
output a directory (rather than a tarball) for the application server bundle. If the output location exists, it will be recursively deleted first
skip building mobile apps even if mobile platforms have been added
set optional data for the initial value of Meteor.settings in your mobile application
location where mobile builds connect to the Meteor server. Defaults to localhost:3000. Can include a URL scheme (for example, --server https://example.com:443)
builds the server for a different architecture than your developer machine's architecture. Note: This option selects the architecture of the binary-dependent At
allow packages in your project to be upgraded or downgraded to versions that are potentially incompatible with the current versions, if required to satisfy all
builds only the specified platforms (when available)
choose between apk/bundle for android builds

subcommands.name: lint
subcommands.description: run through the whole build process for the app and run all linters the app uses

options.description: allow packages in your project to be upgraded or downgraded to versions that are potentially incompatible with the current versions, if required to satisfy all

subcommands.name: shell
subcommands.description: when `meteor shell` is executed in an application directory where a server is already running, it connects to the server and starts an interactive shell for eva

subcommands.name: mongo
subcommands.description: opens a Mongo shell to view or manipulate collections. Can only be used when developing locally

options.description: mongo database URL

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: reset the current project to a fresh state. Removes all local data

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: deploys the project in your current directory to Meteor's servers

options.description: permanently delete this deployment
deploy in debug mode (don't minify, etc)
set optional data for Meteor.settings
allow packages in your project to be upgraded or downgraded to versions that are potentially incompatible with the current versions, if required to satisfy all
the number of milliseconds to wait for build/deploy success or failure after a successful upload of your app's minified code; defaults to 15 minutes
exits when Meteor has uploaded the app's code instead of waiting for the deploy to conclude
reuses the build already created if the git commit hash is the same
when deploying an app for the first time, you can pass this option to deploy your app in the Galaxy's free mode
you can change the app plan by providing this argument with one of the following values: professional, essentials, or free. Be aware that this argument overwrit
if this flag is true and it's not provided a mongo url in the settings ('galaxy.meteor.com'.env.MONGO_URL), when deploying, Galaxy will create a database to you
change your app's container size using the deploy command

states.description: you can deploy to any available name under "meteorapp.com" without any additional configuration, for example, "myapp.meteorapp.com". If you deploy to a custom d

subcommands.name: authorized
subcommands.description: manage authorized users and organizations

options.description: add an authorized user or organization
remove an authorized user or organization
transfer the (Galaxy) app to a new user or organization
list authorized users and organizations

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: prompts for your username and password and logs you in to your Meteor developer account

options.description: add an authorized user or organization

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: log out of your Meteor developer account

subcommands.name: whoami
subcommands.description: prints the username of the currently logged-in Meteor developer

subcommands.name: test-packages
subcommands.description: runs unit tests for one or more packages. The results are shown in a browser dashboard that updates whenever a relevant source file is modified

options.description: specify the release of Meteor to use
port to listen on (instead of the default 3000). Also uses port N+1 and N+2
enable server-side debugging via debugging clients like the Node.js command-line debugger, Chrome DevTools, or Visual Studio Code
enable server-side debugging via debugging clients like the Node.js command-line debugger, Chrome DevTools, or Visual Studio Code. The server will be paused at
location where mobile builds connect to the Meteor server. Defaults to your local IP and the port that the Meteor server binds to. Can include a URL scheme (for
local port where Cordova will serve the content. It's important when multiple Cordova apps are build from the same Meteor app source code as by default the port
simulate production mode. Minify and bundle CSS and JS files
set optional data for Meteor.settings on the server
run tests in an iOS emulator. All of the tests for client and server will run in addition to mobile-specific tests
run tests in an Android emulator. All of the tests for client and server will run in addition to mobile-specific tests
run tests on iOS mobile device. All of the tests for client and server will run in addition to mobile-specific tests
run tests on Android emulator or on a mobile device. All of the tests for client and server will run in addition to mobile-specific tests
set the directory in which to create a temporary app used for tests. Defaults to the system's temporary directory, usually /tmp
print all output from builds logs
don't run linters used by the app on every rebuild
run with additional packages (comma separated, for example: --extra-packages "package-name1, package-name2@1.2.3")
test driver package to use to run tests and display results. For example: --driver-package meteortesting:mocha
package name

subcommands.name: admin
subcommands.description: rarely used commands for administering official Meteor services

subcommands.name: maintainers
subcommands.description: manage users and organizations that are maintainers for a particular package

options.description: add an authorized maintainer to a package
remove an authorized maintainer from a package. You cannot remove yourself if you are the last maintainer on a package
list authorized maintainers

remove a member to the organization
